channel.title: Ottawa COVID-19 Projections on Ottawa COVID19
channel.description: Recent content in Ottawa COVID-19 Projections on Ottawa COVID19

item.title: Methods
item.description:
Described below is a brief description of methods, data and model parameters used at 613covid.ca. More detailed information can be found in the accompanying references.

Projection models

Data used to create hospital projection models can be accessed at Open Ottawa. See ’COVID-19 cases and deaths`. Data used to create wastewater projection models can be accessed at PHESD.

COVID-19 short-term projections are created using the Bayesian EpiNow2 model developed by the EpiForecasts team.

Model use

There are two main uses of the projections:

  1. Short-term projections (1 to 4 weeks) reflect the current level of COVID-19 transmission and current effectiveness of ongoing COVID-19 prevention and control. Short-term projections the immediate need health care such hospital and critical care services for COVID-19 patients.

  2. Long-term projections (6 weeks onward) reflect sustain trends in COVID-19 transmissions. Long-term projections are helpful to assess the potential effect of COVID-19 re-opening or the need of increased restrictions to control surges.

item.title: Changelog
item.description:
V3.6.0

2022-02-24

  • Hospital projections added to homepage.

V3.5.0

2022-01-28

  • Wastewater projections and growth estimates added to Wastewater page.

V3.4.0

2021-06-16

  • Updates to the Wastewater page. Variant plot updated to include a midpoint rolling average. Details on variants of interest/concern detection included in page.

V3.3.3

2021-04-08

  • Plot added to the Wastewater page visualizing proportion of variant B.1.1.7 RNA detected in wastewater.

V3.3.2

2021-03-26

  • Short-term hospitalization projections added.
  • Wastewater Midpoint mean viral signal omits dates flagged by wastewater researchers with potential data concerns.

V3.3.1

2021-03-19

item.title: Contact
item.description:
Media Contact: Dr. Robert Delatolla robert.delatolla@uottawa.ca

Website or data issues? Dr. Lakshmi Pisharody [lpisharo@uottawa.ca] (mailto:lpisharo@uottawa.ca)

Acknowledgements for website and content: Dr. Douglas Manuel, Warsame Yusuf, Rostyslav Vyuha, Yulric Sequeira, Gauri Saran, Mathew Thomson, and others on Big Life Lab team.

Supporting Ottawa Public Health epidemiologists: Amira Ali, Jacqueline Willmore, Dara Spatz

uOttawa — CHEO SARS-CoV-2 research group: Robert Delatolla, Alex MacKenzie, Patrick D’Aoust, Élisabeth Mercier, Antoine Cantin, Tyson Graber

Computing infrastructure: Indoc Research - Open Data Environment

item.title: Ottawa COVID-19 wastewater surveillance
item.description:
613COVID Website Update

Ottawa wastewater surveillance continues with daily testing and analysis performed in the lab of Dr. Robert Delatolla, Professor, Department of Civil Engineering, University of Ottawa, who has led that work since its inception in April 2020.

The lab, led by Dr. Delatolla, has expanded its focus beyond SARS-CoV-2 to include influenza and RSV viruses, with the data being regularly reported on the official website.

Please follow @DelatollaLab on X (Twitter) for further contextualization of the data.

item.title: Ottawa COVID-19 wastewater surveillance
item.description:
Wastewater plots last updated: 25 April, 2026, 12:12:02 PM

Wastewater samples collected up to: 19 April, 2026

Reported date up to: 25 April, 2026

The website is updated Monday to Friday at approximately 5 p.m. Updates can be delayed if measurements do not pass quality assurance and quality control checks.

Samples that are collected from Monday to Thursday are processed the day after collection. The website and plots are updated the day after processing. Samples that are collected from Friday to Sunday are processed on Monday and thus are posted to the website on Tuesday at 5 p.m.

item.title: Ottawa COVID-19 wastewater surveillance (development page)
item.description:
Wastewater plots last updated: 25 April, 2026, 12:12:13 PM

Wastewater samples collected up to: 19 April, 2026

Reported date up to: 25 April, 2026

The website is updated Monday to Friday at approximately 5 p.m. Updates can be delayed if measurements do not pass quality assurance and quality control checks.

Samples that are collected from Monday to Thursday are processed the day after collection. The website and plots are updated the day after processing. Samples that are collected from Friday to Sunday are processed on Monday and thus are posted to the website on Tuesday at 5 p.m.

item.title: Roadmap
item.description:
The main purpose of this site is to visualize Ottawa-based projections for models performed by other teams.

  • Review and update model parameters based on recent published studies. Priories for review:
    • ventilated patients
    • deaths

item.title: Short-term hospital projections for Ottawa (development page)
item.description:
Last updated on 25 April, 2026, 11:48:52 AM

Pour le français cliquez ici.

This website has forecasts of hospitalization counts from COVID-19 and wastewater recovery of SARS-CoV-2.

Website is optimized for desktop use. For mobile users, it is recommended to view in landscape mode.

Click here to access the COVID-19 Daily Dashboard created by Ottawa Public Health. The Ottawa Public Health dashboard provides up to date COVID-19 metrics that inform projections presented on this website.
